description:
You are acting as a QA engineer who has just received three compressed
    web-server access logs.  
    • Location of the compressed log files  
      /home/user/logs/raw/raw01.log.gz  
      /home/user/logs/raw/raw02.log.gz  
      /home/user/logs/raw/raw03.log.gz  

    • Every log file is in “common log format” (one line per request, square-bracket
      timestamp, quoted request, numeric status, etc.).  
    • Each archive contains requests from more than one day, but your manager only
      cares about *01 Oct 2023* and about requests that finished with HTTP status
      *500*.  

    Your tasks are:

    1. Create two new directories that will hold your intermediate and final
       artefacts:  
       /home/user/logs/processed/  
       /home/user/logs/reports/

    2. Uncompress the three *.gz* files into /home/user/logs/processed/.
       You are encouraged to perform the decompression in parallel (e.g. by
       combining xargs ‑P, GNU parallel, background jobs, etc.), but the exact
       method is up to you.

    3. From the decompressed plain-text logs extract **only** those lines that  
       a. were generated on 01-Oct-2023 (UTC) and  
       b. have the final status code 500.  

       • Ignore any request from other dates or with any other status.  
       • Do *not* modify the extracted lines – copy them verbatim.

    4. Using the filtered set, compute:  
       • TOTAL_ERRORS – the number of 500-status lines for 01-Oct-2023 across
         all three logs.  
       • A per-endpoint count, where “endpoint” is the path appearing just after
         the HTTP verb in the quoted request (e.g. /api/login).  

       Sorting rule for the per-endpoint section:  
       – primary: descending by count  
       – secondary (for ties): alphabetical ascending by the endpoint string

    5. Produce two report files inside /home/user/logs/reports/ with **exactly**
       the following formats so the automated test can do a byte-for-byte
       comparison:

       a) /home/user/logs/reports/500_errors_summary.log  
          ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
          DATE: 2023-10-01  
          TOTAL_ERRORS: <integer>  
          ENDPOINT_COUNTS:  
          <endpoint_1> <count_1>  
          <endpoint_2> <count_2>  
          …  
          <endpoint_N> <count_N>  
          ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
          • “DATE:” and “TOTAL_ERRORS:” lines must appear exactly once.  
          • “ENDPOINT_COUNTS:” must be followed by one row per endpoint, in the
            required order.  
          • Use a single space between an endpoint and its count.  
          • No trailing spaces on any line and the file must end with a single
            trailing newline (Unix LF).  

       b) /home/user/logs/reports/500_errors_summary.csv  
          ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
          endpoint,count  
          <endpoint_1>,<count_1>  
          <endpoint_2>,<count_2>  
          …  
          <endpoint_N>,<count_N>  
          ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
          • Include the header line exactly as shown.  
          • Same ordering rules as the *.log* file.  
          • Plain CSV with a single LF at the end.

    6. While working, feel free to use any number of shell commands and utilities
       you like (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, parallel, etc.).  Parallel execution
       is strongly encouraged but *not* mandatory.

    7. When you are finished, you may `cat` the two report files to the console
       so that a user can visually confirm their contents, but make sure the
       files themselves are created in the exact locations stated above.  The
       automated grader will look for those files and verify their byte-level
       contents.

    8. No root or sudo actions are allowed; everything must run as the regular
       user inside the home directory tree specified.

    Summary of what will be checked automatically:
    • /home/user/logs/reports/500_errors_summary.log exists and matches the
      required format exactly.  
    • /home/user/logs/reports/500_errors_summary.csv exists and matches the
      required format exactly.  
    • The numerical values inside both reports correctly reflect the 01-Oct-2023
      status-500 requests contained in the provided log archives.  
    • Endpoint ordering is correct (descending by count, ties broken
      alphabetically).

    Deliverables: the two report files as described above.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing directory tree and files inside the container
        BEFORE the agent starts executing commands
    -->
    Pre-created directories:
        /home/user/logs/
        /home/user/logs/raw/

    Pre-created compressed log files and their *exact* uncompressed contents:

    1. /home/user/logs/raw/raw01.log.gz   (gzip-compressed)
       Uncompressed raw01.log:
       127.0.0.1 - - [30/Sep/2023:23:59:59 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       192.168.1.10 - - [01/Oct/2023:00:00:01 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 500 512 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
       10.0.0.5 - - [01/Oct/2023:00:05:21 +0000] "GET /api/order HTTP/1.1" 500 256 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       10.0.0.8 - - [01/Oct/2023:00:10:44 +0000] "GET /contact.html HTTP/1.1" 200 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       192.168.1.10 - - [01/Oct/2023:01:15:07 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 500 512 "-" "curl/7.68.0"

    2. /home/user/logs/raw/raw02.log.gz
       Uncompressed raw02.log:
       127.0.0.1 - - [01/Oct/2023:02:03:09 +0000] "GET /api/user HTTP/1.1" 500 300 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       127.0.0.1 - - [01/Oct/2023:02:04:10 +0000] "GET /api/user HTTP/1.1" 500 300 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       127.0.0.1 - - [01/Oct/2023:02:05:11 +0000] "GET /api/user HTTP/1.1" 500 300 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       10.0.0.6 - - [01/Oct/2023:03:00:00 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 500 512 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
       10.0.0.7 - - [02/Oct/2023:00:00:01 +0000] "GET /api/user HTTP/1.1" 500 300 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

    3. /home/user/logs/raw/raw03.log.gz
       Uncompressed raw03.log:
       10.0.0.10 - - [01/Oct/2023:04:10:44 +0000] "GET /api/order HTTP/1.1" 500 256 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
       10.0.0.11 - - [01/Oct/2023:05:20:10 +0000] "GET /home HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

    Derived counts that the grader will verify
        TOTAL_ERRORS (01-Oct-2023, status 500) = 8

        Per-endpoint counts (same date & status):
            /api/login 3
            /api/user  3
            /api/order 2

        Order required in both report files:
            1. /api/login (count 3)   <-- tied highest, alphabetically first
            2. /api/user  (count 3)
            3. /api/order (count 2)

    Expected final files and *exact* contents:

    1. /home/user/logs/reports/500_errors_summary.log
       DATE: 2023-10-01
       TOTAL_ERRORS: 8
       ENDPOINT_COUNTS:
       /api/login 3
       /api/user 3
       /api/order 2
       <LF at end>

    2. /home/user/logs/reports/500_errors_summary.csv
       endpoint,count
       /api/login,3
       /api/user,3
       /api/order,2
       <LF at end>
